column2: Notice of public hearings and public meetings on adoption or modification of land use regulation.
Public Hearing Notice - Adoption and Amendment of Design Standards and Public Improvement Specifications.

Notice is hereby given that the American Fork City Planning Commission will hold a public hearing to consider the proposed amendment and adoption of Design Standards and Public Improvement Specifications applicable to development within American Fork City. The proposed Design Standards and Public Improvement Specifications establish engineering, infrastructure, and public improvement requirements for development activity within the City. The proposal includes removing existing engineering standards from the Municipal Code and adopting the new Design Standards and Public Improvement Specifications by reference. The proposed standards are intended to align City requirements with applicable Utah APWA Standard Specifications and Plans while establishing uniform City-specific design criteria. The Planning Commission will receive public comment and make a recommendation to the City Council.

pmn_1059139,Eagle Mountain,Planning Commission,2026-02-24,PCPH Williams Development Agreement,,,https://www.utah.gov/pmn/sitemap/notice/1059139.html,"EAGLE MOUNTAIN CITY
The Eagle Mountain Planning Commission will hold public hearings on February 24, 2026, at
6:30 p.m., or as soon thereafter as business allows, in the Eagle Mountain City Council
Chambers, located at 1650 E. Stagecoach Run, Eagle Mountain, Utah 84005. The Planning
Commission will receive public comments concerning the following items:
Williams Development Agreement: Williams is an independent power producer.
They are looking to co-locate a natural gas power facility on the Stadion parcel to
facilitate the Meta data center expansion. The new natural gas facility would be
located on the Stadion property, which is zoned RTI. Pursuant to the Stadion
development agreement, natural gas power is a permitted accessory use on the
property. Nevertheless, the parties have negotiated a development agreement to
provide additional clarification of the rights and responsibilities of Williams and the
City in relation to the natural gas power facility. Williams would be required to install
any necessary public infrastructure for their development. Williams is asking for a
few exceptions to the city code. Most notably, they are asking to be allowed to go up
to 75 dB in sound. This exceeds the City's nighttime limit, which is 65dB, but is
consistent with industrial noise limits in neighboring communities. Williams is also
asking to be allowed to use above ground power distribution lines on the property.
EMC Point of Contact: City Attorney, Marcus Draper: mdraper@eaglemountain.gov.
Materials for the public hearing items may be viewed within three days of the meeting on the city
website and at the Eagle Mountain City Recorder's Office, 1650 E Stagecoach Run, Eagle Mountain,
UT, during normal business hours, Monday through Friday, 7:30 AM to 5:30 PM. If you need special
accommodation to participate in the meeting, please call the City Recorder's Office at 801.789.6611.

PUBLIC HEARING/ACTION ITEM - Williams Development Agreement

column1: BACKGROUND: (Presented by City Attorney, Marcus Draper) Williams is an independent power producer. They are looking to co-locate a natural gas power facility on the Stadion parcel to facilitate the Meta data center expansion. The new natural gas facility would be located on the Stadion property, which is zoned RTI. Pursuant to the Stadion development agreement, natural gas power is a permitted accessory use on the property. Nevertheless, the parties have negotiated a development agreement to provide additional clarification of the rights and responsibilities of Williams and the City in relation to the natural gas power facility. Williams would be required to install any necessary public infrastructure for their development. Williams is asking for a few exceptions to the city code. Most notably, they are asking to be allowed to go up to 75 dB in sound. This exceeds the City's nighttime limit, which is 65dB, but is consistent with industrial noise limits in neighboring communities. Williams is also asking to be allowed to use above ground power distribution lines on the property.

Eagle Mountain City will hold a public hearing on Tuesday April 7th at 7:00 p.m. at 1650 E Stagecoach Run, Eagle Mountain City, Utah 84005 to discuss the Community Development Block Grant (CDBG) program year July 1, 2026 through June 30, 2027. The amount of CDBG funds for this program year will be discussed along with the range of projects eligible.

The CDBG program is a federally funded program administered by the State of Utah, Department of Workforce Services, Housing and Community Development Division, and provides funding for eligible projects that benefit low- and moderate-income (LMI) persons.

The purpose of this hearing is to provide information about the CDBG program and to receive public input regarding community development needs and potential projects that may be considered for funding. Suggestions will be solicited verbally, electronically, and in writing from all interested parties during the public comment period from March 16 - April 15, 2026. Eligible activities may include public infrastructure improvements, community facilities, housing-related activities, economic development projects, and certain public services.
To comment or for further information, contact Taylor Draney at 801-547-7594 or tdraney@magutah.gov
If Eagle Mountain City chooses to submit a CDBG application, a second public hearing will be held at a later date to review the proposed project.

In accordance with the Americans with Disabilities Act, individuals needing special accommodations should contact Taylor Draney at least five (5) days prior to the hearing. Individuals with hearing or speech impairments may dial 711 (Relay Utah).

Materials for public hearing items may be viewed within three days of the meeting on the city website and at the Eagle Mountain City Recorder's Office, 1650 E Stagecoach Run, Eagle Mountain, UT, during normal business hours, Monday through Friday, 7:30 AM to 5:30 PM. column2: Eagle Mountain has contracted with MHTN to update the Parks, Trails and Open Space Plan. This effort includes a comprehensive inventory of the City's existing parks and recreational assets, an evaluation of current conditions, and an analysis of level of service to identify gaps and opportunities. PUBLIC NOTICE IS HEREBY GIVEN that the Grantsville City Council will hold a Public Hearing on Wednesday, March 25, 2026, at 7:00 p.m. at Grantsville City Hall, located at 429 E. Main St., Grantsville, UT, for the purpose of receiving public input regarding a proposed land exchange between Grantsville City and Dave and Teresa Reed.

Pursuant to the authority granted to municipalities to acquire and dispose of real property, the City Council is considering a proposed land exchange involving certain property owned by Grantsville City and property owned by Dave and Teresa Reed. The proposed exchange involves the transfer of certain real property between the parties. Grantsville City would convey Parcel No. 01-061-0-0070, consisting of approximately (+/-)20.47 acres, to Dave and Teresa Reed. In exchange, Dave and Teresa Reed would convey Parcel No. 01-068-0-0038 and Parcel No. 01-068-0-0055, consisting of approximately (+/-)14.77 acres combined, to Grantsville City.

This meeting will be held electronically via Zoom and in-person at Grantsville City Hall, located at 429 E. Main Street, Grantsville, Utah, 84029. Public comments are welcome and may be made in person or by dialing or logging into Zoom Conferencing using the following:

Public comments may also be sent via email to the City Recorder, Alicia Fairbourne at afairbourne@grantsvilleut.gov. All comments will be distributed to the City Council and entered into public record. Written comments must be received prior 12:00 pm (noon) on the day of the meeting in order to be considered.

column1: pmn_1066785,Grantsville,Grantsville City Council,2026-04-01,Notice of Public Hearing and Bonds to be Issued,,,https://www.utah.gov/pmn/sitemap/notice/1066785.html,"NOTICE OF PUBLIC HEARING AND BONDS TO BE ISSUED

NOTICE IS HEREBY GIVEN pursuant to the provisions of the Local Government Bonding Act, Title 11, Chapter 14, Utah Code Annotated 1953, as amended, (the 'Act'), that on February 4, 2026, the City Council (the 'Council') of Grantsville City, Utah (the 'Issuer'), adopted a resolution (the 'Resolution') in which it authorized the issuance of the Issuer's Sewer Revenue Bonds, Series 2026 (to be issued in one or more series and with such other series or title designation(s) as may be determined by the Issuer) (the 'Bonds') and called a public hearing to receive input from the public with respect to (a) the issuance of the Bonds and (b) any potential economic impact that the Project described herein to be financed with the proceeds of the Bonds may have on the private sector.
TIME, PLACE AND LOCATION OF PUBLIC HEARING

The Issuer shall hold a public hearing on April 1, 2026, at the hour of 7:00 p.m. at 429 East Main Street, Grantsville, Utah.  The purpose of the hearing is to receive input from the public with respect to (a) the issuance of the Bonds and (b) any potential economic impact that the Project (defined herein) to be financed with the proceeds of the Bonds may have on the private sector.  All members of the public are invited to attend and participate.

The Bonds will be issued for the purpose of (a) financing all or a portion of the costs of the construction of improvements to its sewer system and all related improvements (collectively, the 'Project'), (b) funding a deposit to a debt service reserve fund, if necessary, and (c) paying costs of issuance of the Bonds.

The Issuer intends to issue the Bonds in the aggregate principal amount of not more than Fifty Million Dollars ($50,000,000), to mature in not more than thirty-four (34) years from their date or dates, to be sold at a price not less than ninety-eight percent (98%) of the total p",pmn,2026-04-26T03:06:58.271314Z

pmn_1071235,Grantsville,Grantsville City Council,2026-04-07,Notice of Ordinance Adoption,,,https://www.utah.gov/pmn/sitemap/notice/1071235.html,"NOTICE OF ORDINANCE ADOPTION

PUBLIC NOTICE IS HEREBY GIVEN that at the regular Grantsville City Council Meeting held on April 1, 2026, the Grantsville City Council adopted Ordinance 2026-12, which approves an updated sewer rate schedule to ensure adequate funding for the operation, maintenance, and long-term sustainability of the City's wastewater system, including costs associated with a new wastewater treatment facility. The ordinance adopts a multi-year rate structure through Fiscal Year 2035, which includes increases to residential and non-residential base fees and the implementation of usage-based fees beginning in Fiscal Year 2027. The rate adjustments include higher increases in the initial years followed by smaller annual increases thereafter, and are intended to maintain required financial reserves and meet debt service obligations for the City's wastewater infrastructure.

Complete copies of the adopted ordinances are available for review at the Grantsville City Recorder's office during regular business hours and online through the City's website at https://grantsville.municipalcodeonline.com/book?type=orddoc#name=2026 and the Utah Public Notice Website at https://www.utah.gov/pmn/sitemap/publicbody/1840.html.
Published and posted on April 7, 2026 in accordance with UCA § 10-3-711",pmn,2026-04-26T03:06:58.271308Z

column1: pmn_1071659,Grantsville,Grantsville City Council,2026-04-15,Notice of Public Hearing,,,https://www.utah.gov/pmn/sitemap/notice/1071659.html,"GRANTSVILLE CITY NOTICE OF PUBLIC HEARING

PUBLIC NOTICE IS HEREBY GIVEN that the Grantsville City Council will hold a Public Hearing on Wednesday, April 15, 2026, at 7:00 p.m. for the purpose of receiving public input regarding a proposed budget amendment to the General Fund.
The proposed amendment will increase the Capital Projects line item within the Class C Roads Department budget in the amount of $3,037,000.00 for the purchase of road department equipment.

This amendment reflects the use of Class C Road Funds, which are restricted for roadway construction, maintenance, and improvement purposes. No new revenues or external funding sources are being proposed as part of this amendment.
The proposed equipment purchases are intended to improve operational efficiency, reduce long-term maintenance costs, and enhance the City's ability to maintain and preserve roadway infrastructure.

This meeting will be held electronically via Zoom and in-person at Grantsville City Hall, located at 429 E. Main Street, Grantsville, Utah, 84029. Public comments are welcome and may be made in person or by participating through Zoom using the following:

Public comments may also be submitted via email to the City Recorder, Alicia Fairbourne, at afairbourne@grantsvilleut.gov. All comments will be distributed to the City Council and entered into the public record. Comments must be received prior to 12:00 p.m. (noon) on the day of the meeting in order to be considered.

Copies of the proposed resolution and supporting materials are available for public review at the Grantsville City Offices during regular business hours and as part of the City Council meeting materials posted on the City's official website and the Utah Public Notice Website.

column2: Presentation, Public Hearing, Discussion, and Consideration: Consideration of proposed amendments to Grantsville Land Use Ordinance Section 4.5, Lot Standards and Street Frontage.

Presentation, Public Hearing, Discussion, and Consideration: Consideration of an appeal by Teresa Outzs of the Zoning Administrator's denial of a Kennel Permit (six (6) dogs) and a Conditional Use Permit (twelve (12) chickens, hens only) at 176 E Elisabeth Court, located in the R-1-21 zoning district, due to failure to meet open space requirements.
Presentation and Discussion: Discussion of a proposed concept for the Le Property in the Mixed Use (M-U) zoning designation, located at 454 East Main Street.

Presentation and Discussion: Discussion of a proposed General Plan Amendment for parcels 01-059-0-0041, 01-059-0-0001, 01-059-0-0028, and 01-059-0-0027, amending the land use designation from Rural Residential 1 and High Single-Family Residential to High Single-Family Residential for the Orion Subdivision, located approximately at 800 East Main Street.

Presentation and Discussion: Discussion of a proposed Rezone for parcels 01-059-0-0041, 01-059-0-0001, 01-059-0-0028, and 01-059-0-0027, from RR-1, RM-7, to RM-15 for the Orion Subdivision, located approximately at 800 East Main Street.

column2: Review and consider a recommendation to adopt the 2026 Transportation Master Plan Update, which is an element of the adopted Herriamn City General Plan. The 2026 Transportation Master Plan (TMP) update is necessary to address continued population growth, projected to increase from  65,000 residents in 2025 to  115,000 by 2050, and ensure a transportation network that is efficient, connected, and sustainable. The TMP update began in early 2025, led by Wall Consultant Group (WCG). The plan incorporates information from the City's General Plan (Herriman NEXT), regional transportation plans, and community input gathered through surveys and public events, including outreach conducted at Herriman Towne Days. (Public Hearing)

pmn_1068827,Lehi,City Council,2026-04-28,Cedar Hollow Annexation Public Hearing Notice,,,https://www.utah.gov/pmn/sitemap/notice/1068827.html,"PUBLIC NOTICE

On March 10, 2026, the Lehi City Council passed Resolution #2026-10 approving Lehi City's intent to annex approximately 15.31 acres of real property owned by Gary Cooper, located at 400 S. Center Street, Lehi, Utah.

A public hearing has been scheduled before the Lehi City Council on the 28th day of April, 2026, at 7:00 p.m. for the purpose of receiving public comment.  The public hearing will be held at the Lehi Civic Center, in the Council Chambers, located at 131 N. 100 E., Lehi, UT.

The Lehi City Council will annex the above described parcel unless, at or before the public hearing, written protests to the annexation are filed by the owners of real property located within the area proposed for annexation covering a majority of the total private land area within the entire area proposed for annexation and also is equal to at least 1/2 the assessed value of all private real property within the entire area proposed for annexation.",pmn,2026-04-26T03:06:58.271842Z

column1: pmn_1070499,Lehi,City Council,2026-04-28,Public Hearing Notice for the Lehi City Community Development Block Grant,,,https://www.utah.gov/pmn/sitemap/notice/1070499.html,"Public Hearing and Public Comment Period Notice
Lehi City Community Development Block Grant

Mountainland Association of Governments will hold a public hearing to consider feedback on the Lehi City CDBG Annual Action Plan, which outlines community needs, potential community funding priorities, and program structure and governance for the program year 2026-2027. The amount of CDBG funds for this program year will be discussed along with the range of projects eligible.

The hearing will be held in the Lehi Council Chambers located at 131 North 100 East, Lehi, Utah, on April 28th at 7:00 pm. Suggestions will be solicited verbally, electronically, and in writing from all interested parties during the public comment period from April 3 to May 5, 2026. To comment or for further information, contact Claudia Saldana at 801-229-3673 or csaldana@mountainland.org.

The public hearing facility is fully accessible to persons with disabilities. Individuals needing special accommodations (including auxiliary aids/services or translation) during the hearing should notify Claudia Saldana at least five days prior to the hearing. A Utah Relay telephone number or a language interpreter can be provided upon request.

Mountainland Association of Governments llevará a cabo una audiencia pública para considerar la retroalimentación sobre el Plan de Acción Anual de la Ciudad de Lehi CDBG, que resume las necesidades de la comunidad, posibles prioridades de financiación comunitaria y la estructura y gestión del programa para el año 2026-2027. El monto estimado de los fondos CDBG para este año del programa se examinará junto con la gama de proyectos elegibles.

La audiencia se llevará a cabo en las oficinas de la ciudad de Lehi, 131 North 100 East, Lehi, Utah, el Martes 28 de Abril de 2026 a las 7:00 pm. Las sugerencias serán solicitadas verbalmente, electrónicamente y por escrito de todas las partes interesadas durante el período de comentarios públi",pmn,2026-04-26T03:06:58.271848Z

Notice is hereby given that the South Jordan Planning Commission will hold a meeting at 6:30 p.m. on Tuesday, March 10, 2026. The meeting will be conducted in person in the City Council Chambers, located at 1600 W. Towne Center Drive, South Jordan, Utah, and virtually via Zoom phone and video conferencing. Persons with disabilities requesting assistance should contact the City Recorder at least 24 hours prior to the meeting. Times listed are approximate and may be accelerated or delayed.

In addition to in-person attendance, individuals may join via phone or video using Zoom. Please note that attendees joining virtually or by phone may not comment during public comment or a public hearing; to comment, individuals must attend in person.

If the meeting is disrupted in any way deemed inappropriate by the City, the City reserves the right to immediately remove the individual(s) from the meeting and, if necessary, end virtual access to the meeting. Reasons for removal or ending virtual access include, but are not limited to, posting offensive pictures or remarks, making disrespectful statements or actions, and other actions deemed inappropriate.
To ensure that comments are received, please submit them in writing to City Planner, Greg Schindler at gschindler@sjc.utah.gov by 3:00 p.m. on the day of the meeting.
Instructions on how to join virtually are provided below.
Join South Jordan Planning Commission Electronic Meeting:
-           Join on any device that has internet capability.
-           Zoom link, Meeting ID and Password will be provided 24 hours prior to meeting start time.
THE MEETING WILL BEGIN AT 6:30 P.M. AND THE AGENDA IS AS FOLLOWS:

column1: pmn_1062943,South Jordan,City Council,2026-05-05,"AMENDED NOTICE OF PUBLIC HEARING REGARDING THE CREATION OF A PUBLIC INFRASTRUCTURE DISTRICT BY SOUTH JORDAN CITY, UTAH",,,https://www.utah.gov/pmn/sitemap/notice/1062943.html,"NOTICE OF PUBLIC HEARING REGARDING THE CREATION OF A PUBLIC INFRASTRUCTURE DISTRICT BY SOUTH JORDAN CITY, UTAH

Notice is hereby given that the City of South Jordan City Council (the 'Council') shall hold a public hearing on Tuesday, May 5, 2026 at 6:30 p.m. The public hearing is regarding the proposed creation of the So Jo Marketplace Public Infrastructure District (the 'Proposed District') and to allow for public input on (i) whether the requested service (described below) are needed in the area of the Proposed District, (ii) whether the service should be provided by the City or the Proposed District, and (iii) all other matters relating to the Proposed District.

Because consent to the creation of the Proposed District and waiver of the protest period has been obtained from all property owners and registered voters within the boundaries of the Proposed District, pursuant to Utah Code, Section 17D-4-201, the City may adopt a resolution creating the Proposed District immediately after holding the public hearing described herein or on any date thereafter. Any withdrawal of consent to creation or protest of the creation of the Proposed District by an affected property owner must be submitted to the City prior to the public hearing described herein.
Held By: The City of South Jordan City Council
Date and Time: Tuesday, May 5, 2026 at 6:30 p.m.
Location: South Jordan City Hall - City Council Chambers 1600 W. Towne Center Drive, South Jordan City, UT 84095
To view Council meeting, live, visit the 'South Jordan City Council Website' https://www.sjc.utah.gov/241/City-Council

A legal description for the Proposed District is attached as Appendix A. In addition, it is anticipated that the Proposed District will be authorized to adjust its boundaries through annexation and withdrawal of properties, so long as such properties are within the proposed inclusion area, as shown on the map attached as Appendix B an",pmn,2026-04-26T03:06:58.269533Z
